calendar.matters:
Motion For New Trial And Memorandum In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Sep-20-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Albert J. Garcia, Esq., Tel. 510.848.5190, appearing for Plaintiff Guven Uzun, also appearing via Zoom;
Greg W. Chambers, Esq., Supervising Deputy Attorney General, appearing for the Medical Board of California.

Hearing re: PETITIONER GUVEN UZUN'S MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL.

This matter is contested. Argument is held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Petitioner's motion for new trial is denied. Petitioner fails to show an irregularity in the proceedings, insufficiency of the evidence, or an error of law. (CCP 657(1), (6) and (7).)

Pursuant to Business and Professions Code section 2307(a) ("B&P"), petitioner sought to have his physician's and surgeon's certificate reinstated. In such a proceeding, "[t]he panel of the board or the administrative law judge hearing the petition may consider all activities of the petitioner since the disciplinary action was taken, the offense for which the petitioner was disciplined, the petitioner's activities during the time the certificate was in good standing, and the petitioner's rehabilitative efforts, general reputation for truth, and professional ability." (B&P 2307(e); see also 16 California Code of Regulations 1360.2(b) [criteria in evaluating rehabilitation].)

Here, petitioner argued that he was rehabilitated, but the record showed that he continued to deny wrongdoing. (Exhibit 33, Z68, Z73.) The record supported respondent's conclusion that "the evidence leaves substantial doubts about petitioner's professed acceptance of responsibility and his fitness to resume the practice of medicine." (Exhibit 18, p. 17.) The feigned atonement is inconsistent with the physician-relationship relationship, which is built on trust.

Petitioner correctly notes that the decision references petitioner's mental fitness, an issue that petitioner raised by presenting the report from Dr. Fisher. Respondent's comments on subject were superfluous and do not warrant a remand. Again, substantial evidence supports the decision because petitioner failed to accept responsibility for his wrongdoing and respondent reasonably found a lack of candor/rehabilitation. While petitioner may object to some of the language in the decision, a petition for administrative mandate is not a vehicle to attack purportedly objectionable language in a decision.

The Court will review the proposed order submitted by Mr. Chambers.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Carol Lynn Cox, CSR # 5128, Tel. 714.336.1430, Email: Carollynn@aol.com. Bailiff: Not Present. (301/RCE)

Motion For New Trial And Memorandum In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Sep-06-2024 Continued To Sep-20-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Hearing re: PETITIONER GUVEN UZUN'S Motion For New Trial.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Continued to September 20, 2024, to allow compliance with SF Local Rule 2.7B (courtesy copies). The court only received papers of Mr. Uzun's reply. Please immediately provide the courtesy copies with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: Nguyen. (301/RCE)
